machine.name: Benefits Eligibility Screener
machine.description: Monitors income threshold compliance, residency documentation status, categorical eligibility criteria, and application completeness for community benefits programs (SNAP, LIHEAP, housing assistance, childcare subsidies). Tracks the eligibility determination workflow from initial screening through confirmation, identifying documentation gaps and conditional holds before they stall service delivery. Output at [430:434] feeds the Enrollment Coordination Monitor.

metadata.author: Reality Engine
metadata.populationFocus: Applicants for means-tested community benefits programs; newly unemployed; families with recent income changes; aging-out youth; immigrants with pending status

sensorNormalization.income_threshold_norm: 0.0=income clearly above program threshold (ineligible), 0.5=income near threshold boundary (requires calculation), 1.0=income clearly within program limits
sensorNormalization.residency_documentation_norm: 0.0=no valid residency documentation (no ID, no address proof, no utility bill), 0.5=partial documentation (one of two required documents), 1.0=all residency documentation complete and valid
sensorNormalization.categorical_eligibility_norm: 0.0=no categorical qualifier met (not disabled, not elderly, no minor children, not pregnant), 0.5=one categorical qualifier pending verification, 1.0=categorical eligibility confirmed (SSI recipient, Medicaid enrolled, TANF active)
sensorNormalization.application_completeness_norm: 0.0=application not started or less than 50% complete, 0.5=application submitted but key fields missing, 1.0=application complete and all required fields populated

sequences.name: Eligibility Determination: ELIGIBLE+ → BORDERLINE → INELIGIBLE → ELIGIBILITY_DENIED

metadata.description: Tracks eligibility deterioration from a confirmed eligible baseline through income or documentation threshold crossings. In practice this sequence fires when a beneficiary's income changes (raise, new job, inheritance) or a categorical qualifier expires (child ages out, disability determination reversed). The ELIGIBLE+ baseline ensures at least one confirmed eligible period before denial can be signaled — preventing false negatives on initial applications.

metadata.name: ELIGIBLE
metadata.description: ELIGIBLE+ — income within limits, residency documented, categorical qualifier confirmed, application complete. isInitial A*A = A+.

metadata.name: BORDERLINE
metadata.description: BORDERLINE — income approaching or slightly above threshold; documentation partially lapsing; categorical qualifier under review.

metadata.name: INELIGIBLE
metadata.description: INELIGIBLE — income clearly over threshold OR categorical qualifier lost OR residency documentation expired. Fires ELIGIBILITY_DENIED.
ELIGIBILITY_DENIED: applicant does not meet program criteria at [430:434]
metadata.action: Denial notice with appeal rights; alternative program screening; benefits navigator referral for near-miss cases

metadata.description: Fires DOCUMENTATION_NEEDED when income and categorical eligibility are met but application is stalled by missing documentation — the most common reason eligible households are not enrolled. Targets the gap between eligibility and enrollment.

metadata.name: DOCS_PENDING
metadata.description: DOCS_PENDING+ — income qualifies, categorical eligibility met, but residency documentation incomplete or application has missing fields. isInitial A*A = A+: fires DOCUMENTATION_NEEDED each cycle.
metadata.scenario: Applicant has pay stubs and SSI letter but cannot produce current lease or utility bill after recent move

metadata.description: DOCUMENTATION_NEEDED: applicant is income-eligible but missing required documentation at [430:434]
metadata.action: Document checklist provision; benefits navigator document collection support; alternative documentation acceptance review; 30-day cure period activation

metadata.description: Fires ELIGIBILITY_CONFIRMED when all four eligibility dimensions are above threshold — income qualifies, residency documented, categorical qualifier confirmed, and application complete. Downstream enrollment coordination monitor uses this signal to trigger active enrollment workflow.

metadata.name: CONFIRMED
metadata.description: CONFIRMED+ — all eligibility criteria met; documentation complete; application verified and ready for enrollment. Fires ELIGIBILITY_CONFIRMED each cycle.
metadata.scenario: SNAP applicant: income verified at 95% FPL, current lease provided, application complete — ready for 30-day determination

metadata.description: ELIGIBILITY_CONFIRMED: all criteria met; proceed to enrollment at [430:434]
metadata.action: Enrollment coordination monitor receives ELIGIBILITY_CONFIRMED; benefit issuance workflow initiated

inputSequences.name: Job Loss to SNAP Enrollment
inputSequences.pattern: 5-step: ELIGIBLE→ELIGIBLE→DOCS_PENDING (income verified but pay stub needed)→CONFIRMED→CONFIRMED
inputSequences.description: Recently unemployed applicant; income clearly within limits after layoff; missing final pay stub initially; navigator assists with documentation; eligibility confirmed.
